title: viola canina var. multicaulis
intro: Viola canina var. multicaulis is a variety of the dog violet plant, characterized by its multiple stems and leaves. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Viola canina var. multicaulis are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as acting as an expectorant to ease respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Viola canina var. multicaulis can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
